## Summary: Author Name Rendering in Jekyll al-folio Bibliography

Based on my thorough exploration of your Jekyll academic website, here's how author names flow from the .bib file to rendered HTML:

### 1. **Bibliography Source File**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_bibliography/papers.bib`

Author names are stored in standard BibTeX format:
- Lines 6, 24, 40, 64, 89: Author names appear in the `author={}` field
- Format examples:
  - `author={Huang, Zilin and Wan, Zhengyang and Sheng, Zihao and ...}`
  - `author={Wan, Zhengyang and Zhou, Hechao and Zhang✉, Jimin}`
- Metadata annotations use symbols: `*` for equal contribution, `✉` for corresponding author

### 2. **Jekyll Scholar Configuration**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_config.yml` (lines 314-337)

The scholar configuration identifies **your name** for self-highlighting:
```yaml
scholar:
  last_name: [Wan]
  first_name: [Zhengyang]
  
  style: apa
  locale: en
  source: /_bibliography/
  bibliography: papers.bib
  bibliography_template: bib
```

Additional settings affecting author display:
- Line 377: `max_author_limit: 3` — Limits visible authors before "more authors" link
- Line 378: `more_authors_animation_delay: 10` — Animation speed for author list reveal

### 3. **Core Author Rendering Logic**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 54-148)

The rendering flow:

**Lines 63-70:** Self-identification logic
```liquid
{% assign author_is_self = false %}
{%- assign author_last_name = author.last | regex_replace: '[*∗†‡§¶‖&^]', '' -%}
{% if site.scholar.last_name contains author_last_name %}
  {% if site.scholar.first_name contains author.first %}
    {% assign author_is_self = true %}
  {% endif %}
{% endif %}
```
- Strips symbols from author last names for comparison
- Checks if author matches `site.scholar.last_name` (Wan) AND `site.scholar.first_name` (Zhengyang)

**Lines 71-80:** Coauthor profile URL lookup
```liquid
{% assign coauthor_url = null %}
{%- assign clean_last_name = author_last_name | downcase | remove_accents -%}
{% if site.data.coauthors[clean_last_name] %}
  {%- for coauthor in site.data.coauthors[clean_last_name] -%}
    {% if coauthor.firstname contains author.first %}
      {%- assign coauthor_url = coauthor.url -%}
    {% endif %}
  {% endfor %}
{% endif %}
```
- Looks up author profiles in `_data/coauthors.yml`

**Lines 86-101:** Conditional rendering
```liquid
{%- if author_is_self -%}
  <em>{{- author.first }} {{ author_last_html -}}</em>
{%- else -%}
  {%- if coauthor_url -%}
    <a href="{{ coauthor_url }}">{{- author.first }} {{ author_last_html -}}</a>
  {%- else -%}

  {% endif %}
{%- endif -%}
```

- **Known coauthors:** Rendered as `<a>` links to their profiles
- **Unknown authors:** Plain text

**Lines 103-137:** "More authors" collapsible list for when `max_author_limit` is exceeded

### Key Flow Summary:
1. **BibTeX parsing:** jekyll-scholar parses `papers.bib` 
2. **Self-detection:** bib.liquid compares each author against `_config.yml` scholar settings
3. **Profile lookup:** Attempts to find author URLs in `_data/coauthors.yml`
4. **Rendering:** Outputs `<em>` (self), `<a>` (linked coauthors), or plain text
5. **Styling:** CSS applies different borders/underlines based on element type
6. **Symbol handling:** Superscripts for contributions/affiliation are preserved visually

Your current setup automatically italicizes and underlines **Zhengyang Wan** on all publications. To bold your name instead, you would modify the `<em>` rendering in bib.liquid (line 87-90) to use `<strong>` tags or add a custom CSS class.

152	    background-color: var(--global-danger-block-bg);
153	
154	    em,
155	    li,
156	    p,
157	    strong {
158	      color: var(--global-danger-block-text);
159	    }
160	
161	    h1,
162	    h2,
163	    h3,
164	    h4,
165	    h5,
166	    h6 {
167	      color: var(--global-danger-block-title);
168	    }
169	  }
170	}
171	
172	// Checklist
173	
174	ul.task-list {
175	  list-style-type: none; /* Remove bullets from all levels */
176	}
177	
178	ul.task-list > li ul {
179	  /* Nested lists within .task-list */
180	  padding-inline-start: 1.5rem;
181	}
182	
183	ul.task-list input[type="checkbox"] {
184	  margin: 0.275rem 0.5rem 0.5rem -1rem;
185	  vertical-align: middle;
186	}
187	
188	// Math
189	
190	.equation {
191	  margin-bottom: 1rem;
192	  text-align: center;
193	}
194	
195	// Caption
196	
197	.caption {
198	  font-size: 0.875rem;
199	  margin-top: 0.75rem;
200	  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
201	  text-align: center;
202	}
203	
204	// Card
205	
206	.card {
207	  background-color: var(--global-card-bg-color);
208	
209	  .card-img {
210	    width: 100%;
211	    padding-top: 1.25rem;
212	  }
213	
214	  .card-title {
215	    color: var(--global-text-color);
216	  }
217	
218	  .card-body {
219	    padding: 1.25rem 1.25rem 1.25rem 1.25rem;
220	  }
221	}
222	
223	// Citation
224	
225	.citation,
226	.citation-number {
227	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
228	}
229	
230	// Profile
231	
232	.profile {
233	  width: 100%;
234	
235	  .more-info {
236	    margin-bottom: 5px;
237	    margin-top: 5px;
238	    font-family: monospace;
239	
240	    p {
241	      display: inline-block;
242	      margin: 0;
243	    }
244	  }
245	}
246	
247	.profile.float-right {
248	  margin-left: 1rem;
249	}
250	
251	.profile.float-left {
252	  margin-right: 1rem;
253	}
254	
255	@media (min-width: 576px) {
256	  .profile {
257	    width: 30%;
258	
259	    .address {
260	      p {
261	        display: block;
262	      }
263	    }
264	  }
265	}
266	
267	.post-description {
268	  margin-bottom: 2rem;
269	  font-size: 0.875rem;
270	
271	  a {
272	    color: inherit;
273	
274	    &:hover {
275	      color: var(--global-theme-color);
276	      text-decoration: none;
277	    }
278	  }
279	}
280	
281	// Navbar customization
282	
283	.navbar {
284	  box-shadow: none;
285	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
286	  background-color: var(--global-bg-color);
287	  opacity: 0.95;
288	}
289	
290	.navbar .dropdown-menu {
291	  background-color: var(--global-bg-color);
292	  border: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
293	
294	  a:not(.active) {
295	    color: var(--global-text-color);
296	  }
297	
298	  a:hover {
299	    color: var(--global-hover-color);
300	  }
301	
302	  .dropdown-divider {
303	    border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color) !important;
304	  }
305	}
306	
307	.dropdown-item {
308	  color: var(--global-text-color);
309	
310	  &:hover {
311	    color: var(--global-hover-color);
312	    background-color: var(--global-bg-color);
313	  }
314	}
315	
316	.navbar.navbar-light {
317	  a {
318	    &:hover {
319	      text-decoration: none;
320	    }
321	  }
322	
323	  .navbar-brand {
324	    color: var(--global-text-color);
325	  }
326	
327	  .navbar-nav .nav-item .nav-link {
328	    color: var(--global-text-color);
329	
330	    &:hover {
331	      color: var(--global-hover-color);
332	    }
333	  }
334	
335	  .navbar-nav .nav-item.active > .nav-link {
336	    background-color: inherit;
337	    font-weight: bolder;
338	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
339	
340	    &:hover {
341	      color: var(--global-hover-color);
342	    }
343	  }
344	
345	  .navbar-brand.social {
346	    padding-bottom: 0;
347	    padding-top: 0;
348	    font-size: 1.7rem;
349	
350	    a {
351	      i::before {
352	        color: var(--global-text-color);
353	        transition-property: all 0.2s ease-in-out;
354	      }
355	
356	      &:hover {
357	        i::before {
358	          color: var(--global-theme-color);
359	        }
360	      }
361	    }
362	  }
363	}
364	
365	.navbar-toggler {
366	  .icon-bar {
367	    display: block;
368	    width: 22px;
369	    height: 2px;
370	    background-color: var(--global-text-color);
371	    border-radius: 1px;
372	    margin-bottom: 4px;
373	    transition: all 0.2s;
374	  }
375	
376	  .top-bar {
377	    transform: rotate(45deg);
378	    transform-origin: 10% 10%;
379	  }
380	
381	  .middle-bar {
382	    opacity: 0;
383	  }
384	
385	  .bottom-bar {
386	    transform: rotate(-45deg);
387	    transform-origin: 10% 90%;
388	  }
389	}
390	
391	.navbar-toggler.collapsed {
392	  .top-bar {
393	    transform: rotate(0);
394	  }
395	
396	  .middle-bar {
397	    opacity: 1;
398	  }
399	
400	  .bottom-bar {
401	    transform: rotate(0);
402	  }
403	}
404	
405	#light-toggle,
406	#search-toggle {
407	  padding: 0;
408	  border: 0;
409	  background-color: inherit;
410	  color: var(--global-text-color);
411	
412	  &:hover {
413	    color: var(--global-hover-color);
414	  }
415	}
416	
417	// Social (bottom)
418	
419	.social {
420	  text-align: left;
421	
422	  .contact-icons {
423	    font-size: 2rem;
424	    margin-bottom: 5px;
425	
426	    a {
427	      i::before {
428	        color: var(--global-text-color);
429	        transition-property: all 0.2s ease-in-out;
430	      }
431	
432	      &:hover {
433	        i::before {
434	          color: var(--global-hover-color);
435	        }
436	      }
437	    }
438	  }
439	
440	  .contact-note {
441	    font-size: 0.8rem;
442	  }
443	}
444	
445	.wechat-modal {
446	  display: none;
447	  position: fixed;
448	  z-index: 1;
449	  padding-top: 100px;
450	  left: 0;
451	  top: 0;
452	  width: 100%;
453	  height: 100%;
454	  overflow: auto;
455	  background-color: rgb(0, 0, 0);
456	  background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
457	}
458	
459	.wechat-modal > img {
460	  display: block;
461	  position: relative;
462	  top: 50%;
463	  left: 50%;
464	  width: 33%;
465	  max-width: 400px;
466	  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
467	}
468	
469	@media only screen and (max-width: 400px) {
470	  .wechat-modal > img {
471	    width: 100%;
472	  }
473	}
474	
475	// Footer
476	footer.fixed-bottom {
477	  background-color: var(--global-footer-bg-color);
478	  font-size: 0.75rem;
479	
480	  .container {
481	    color: var(--global-footer-text-color);
482	    padding-top: 9px;
483	    padding-bottom: 8px;
484	    text-align: center;
485	  }
486	
487	  a {
488	    color: var(--global-footer-link-color);
489	
490	    &:hover {
491	      color: var(--global-theme-color);
492	      text-decoration: none;
493	    }
494	  }
495	}
496	
497	footer.sticky-bottom {
498	  border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
499	  padding-top: 40px;
500	  padding-bottom: 40px;
501	  font-size: 0.9rem;

503	  .container {
504	    text-align: center;
505	  }

509

512	
513	  .card {

517	    .list-group-item {

521	      .badge {

541

549

554	  padding-bottom: 3rem;

556	  h1 {
557	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
558	    font-size: 5rem;
559	  }
560	}

562	.tag-category-list {

567	  ul {

571	    p,
572	    li {

578	  }

584	  }
585	}

587	.post-list {
588	  margin: 0;
589	  margin-bottom: 40px;
590	  padding: 0;
591	
592	  li {

594	    list-style: none;

596	    padding-bottom: 2rem;

598	    .post-meta {

604	    .post-tags {

610	
611	    a {

614

619

622	    }

632	      &:hover {

634	      }
635	    }

640	
641	      &:hover {

643	      }
644	    }

649	
650	.distill {

652	    border-bottom-color: var(--global-theme-color);
653	    text-decoration: none;
654	  }
655	}
656	
657	// Projects
658	
659	.projects {
660	  a {
661	    text-decoration: none;
662	
663	    &:hover {
664	      .card-title {
665	        color: var(--global-theme-color);
666	      }
667	    }
668	  }
669	
670	  .card {
671	    img {
672	      width: 100%;
673	    }
674	  }
675	
676	  .col {
677	    padding-bottom: 1rem;
678	  }
679	
680	  .grid-sizer,
681	  .grid-item {
682	    width: 250px;
683	    margin-bottom: 10px;
684	  }
685	
686	  h2.category {
687	    color: var(--global-divider-color);
688	    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
689	    padding-top: 0.5rem;
690	    margin-top: 2rem;
691	    margin-bottom: 1rem;
692	    text-align: right;
693	  }
694	}
695	
696	// Publications
697	
698	.publications {
699	  margin-top: 2rem;
700	
701	  h1 {
702	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
703	    font-size: 2rem;
704	    text-align: center;
705	    margin-top: 1em;
706	    margin-bottom: 1em;
707	  }
708	
709	  h2 {
710	    margin-bottom: 1rem;
711	
712	    span {
713	      font-size: 1.5rem;
714	    }
715	  }
716	
717	  h2.bibliography {
718	    color: var(--global-divider-color);
719	    border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
720	    padding-top: 1rem;
721	    margin-top: 2rem;
722	    text-align: right;
723	  }
724	
725	  ol.bibliography {
726	    list-style: none;
727	    padding: 0;
728	    margin-top: 0;
729	
730	    li {
731	      margin-bottom: 1rem;
732	
733	      .preview {
734	        width: 100%;
735	        height: auto;
736	        aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
737	        object-fit: cover;
738	      }
739	
740	      .abbr {
741	        margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
742	
743	        abbr {
744	          display: inline-block;
745	          background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
746	          margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
747	          color: white;
748	
749	          a {
750	            color: inherit;
751	
752	            &:hover {
753	              text-decoration: none;
754	            }
755	          }
756	        }
757	
758	        .award {
759	          color: var(--global-theme-color) !important;
760	          border: 1px solid var(--global-theme-color);
761	        }
762	      }
763	
764	      .title {
765	        font-size: 1.1rem;
766	        font-weight: 700;
767	        line-height: 1.35;
768	      }
769	
770	      .author {
771	        font-size: 0.9rem;
772	
773	        a {
774	          border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-theme-color);
775	
776	          &:hover {
777	            border-bottom-style: solid;
778	            text-decoration: none;
779	          }
780	        }
781	
782	        > em {
783	          font-weight: 400;
784	          border-bottom: 1px solid;
785	          font-style: normal;
786	        }
787	
788	        > span.more-authors {
789	          color: var(--global-text-color-light);
790	          border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-text-color-light);
791	          cursor: pointer;
792	
793	          &:hover {
794	            color: var(--global-text-color);
795	            border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-text-color);
796	          }
797	        }
798	      }
799	
800	      .links {
801	        a.btn {
802	          color: var(--global-text-color);
803	          border: 1px solid var(--global-text-color);
804	          padding-left: 1rem;
805	          padding-right: 1rem;
806	          padding-top: 0.25rem;
807	          padding-bottom: 0.25rem;
808	          margin-left: 0;
809	
810	          &:hover {
811	            color: var(--global-theme-color);
812	            border-color: var(--global-theme-color);
813	          }
814	        }
815	        a.award.btn {
816	          border-color: var(--global-highlight-color);
817	        }
818	      }
819	
820	      .badges {
821	        padding-bottom: 0.5rem;
822	        span {
823	          display: inline-block;
824	          color: $black-color;
825	          height: 100%;
826	          padding-right: 0.5rem;
827	          vertical-align: middle;
828	
829	          &:hover {
830	            text-decoration: underline;
831	          }
832	        }
833	      }
834	
835	      .hidden {
836	        font-size: 0.875rem;
837	        max-height: 0px;
838	        overflow: hidden;
839	        text-align: justify;
840	        transition-property: 0.15s ease;
841	        -moz-transition: 0.15s ease;
842	        -ms-transition: 0.15s ease;
843	        -o-transition: 0.15s ease;
844	        transition: all 0.15s ease;
845	
846	        p {
847	          line-height: 1.4em;
848	          margin: 10px;
849	        }
850	
851	        pre {
852	          font-size: 1em;
853	          line-height: 1.4em;
854	          padding: 10px;
855	        }
856	      }
857	
858	      .hidden.open {
859	        max-height: 100em;
860	        transition-property: 0.15s ease;
861	        -moz-transition: 0.15s ease;
862	        -ms-transition: 0.15s ease;
863	        -o-transition: 0.15s ease;
864	        transition: all 0.15s ease;
865	      }
866	
867	      div.abstract.hidden {
868	        border: dashed 1px var(--global-bg-color);
869	      }
870	
871	      div.abstract.hidden.open {
872	        border-color: var(--global-text-color);
873	      }
874	    }
875	    div.award.hidden {
876	      border: dashed 1px var(--global-bg-color);
877	    }
878	    div.award.hidden.open {
879	      border-color: var(--global-highlight-color);
880	    }
881	  }
882	}
883	
884	// Rouge Color Customization
885	figure.highlight {
886	  margin: 0 0 1rem;
887	}
888	
889	pre {
890	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
891	  background-color: var(--global-code-bg-color);
892	  border-radius: 6px;
893	  padding: 6px 12px;
894	
895	  pre,
896	  code {
897	    background-color: transparent;
898	    border-radius: 0;
899	    margin-bottom: 0;
900	    padding: 0;
901	  }
902	}
903	
904	code {
905	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
906	  background-color: var(--global-code-bg-color);
907	  border-radius: 3px;
908	  padding: 3px 3px;
909	}
910	
911	// Transitioning Themes
912	html.transition,
913	html.transition *,
914	html.transition *:before,
915	html.transition *:after {
916	  transition: all 750ms !important;
917	  transition-delay: 0 !important;
918	}
919	
920	// Extra Markdown style (post Customization)
921	.post {
922	  .post-meta {
923	    color: var(--global-text-color-light);
924	    font-size: 0.875rem;
925	    margin-bottom: 0;
926	  }
927	
928	  .post-tags {
929	    color: var(--global-text-color-light);
930	    font-size: 0.875rem;
931	    padding-top: 0.25rem;
932	    padding-bottom: 1rem;
933	
934	    a {
935	      color: var(--global-text-color-light);
936	      text-decoration: none;
937	
938	      &:hover {
939	        color: var(--global-theme-color);
940	      }
941	    }
942	  }
943	}
944	
945	progress {
946	  /* Positioning */
947	  position: fixed;
948	  left: 0;
949	  top: 56px;
950	  z-index: 10;
951	
952	  /* Dimensions */
953	  width: 100%;
954	  height: 1px;
955	
956	  /* Reset the appearance */
957	  -webkit-appearance: none;
958	  -moz-appearance: none;
959	  appearance: none;
960	
961	  /* Get rid of the default border in Firefox/Opera. */
962	  border: none;
963	
964	  /* Progress bar container for Firefox/IE10  */
965	  background-color: transparent;
966	
967	  /* Progress bar value for IE10  */
968	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
969	}
970	
971	progress::-webkit-progress-bar {
972	  background-color: transparent;
973	}
974	
975	progress::-webkit-progress-value {
976	  background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
977	}
978	
979	progress::-moz-progress-bar {
980	  background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
981	}
982	
983	.progress-container {
984	  width: 100%;
985	  background-color: transparent;
986	  position: fixed;
987	  top: 56px;
988	  left: 0;
989	  height: 5px;
990	  display: block;
991	}
992	
993	.progress-bar {
994	  background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
995	  width: 0%;
996	  display: block;
997	  height: inherit;
998	}
999	
1000	/* Copy button */
1001	.code-display-wrapper {
1002	  position: relative;
1003	
1004	  .copy {
1005	    background: var(--global-card-bg-color);
1006	    border-color: var(--global-bg-color);
1007	    border-radius: 0.3rem;
1008	    border-style: none;
1009	    color: var(--global-text-color);
1010	    font-size: medium;
1011	    opacity: 0;
1012	    position: absolute;
1013	    right: 0.2rem;
1014	    top: 0.2rem;
1015	  }
1016

315	  last_name: [Wan]
316	  first_name: [Zhengyang]
317	
318	  style: apa
319	  locale: en
320	
321	  source: /_bibliography/
322	  bibliography: papers.bib
323	  bibliography_template: bib
324	  # Note: if you have latex math in your bibtex, the latex filter
325	  # preprocessing may conflict with MathJAX if the latter is enabled.
326	  # See https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/357.
327	  bibtex_filters: [latex, smallcaps, superscript]
328	
329	  replace_strings: true
330	  join_strings: true
331	
332	  details_dir: bibliography
333	  details_link: Details
334	
335	  query: "@*"
336	  group_by: year
337	  group_order: descending
338	
339	# Display different badges withs stats for your publications
340	# Customize badge behavior in _layouts/bib.liquid
341	enable_publication_badges:
342	  altmetric: true # Altmetric badge (Customization options: https://badge-docs.altmetric.com/index.html)
343	  dimensions: true # Dimensions badge (Customization options: https://badge.dimensions.ai/)
344	  google_scholar: true # Google Scholar badge (https://scholar.google.com/intl/en/scholar/citations.html)
345	  github_stars: true # GitHub stars badge for papers with associated repos
346	  inspirehep: true # Inspire HEP badge (https://help.inspirehep.net/knowledge-base/citation-metrics/)
347	
348	# Filter out certain bibtex entry keywords used internally from the bib output
349	filtered_bibtex_keywords:
350	  [
351	    abbr,
352	    abstract,
353	    additional_info,
354	    altmetric,
355	    annotation,
356	    arxiv,

358	    award_name,

369	    selected,
370	    slides,
371	    supp,
372	    video,
373	    website,
374	  ]
375	
376	# Maximum number of authors to be shown for each publication (more authors are visible on click)
377	max_author_limit: 3 # leave blank to always show all authors
378	more_authors_animation_delay: 10 # more authors are revealed on click using animation; smaller delay means faster animation
379	
380	# Enables publication thumbnails. If disabled, none of the publications will display thumbnails, even if specified in the bib entry.
381	enable_publication_thumbnails: true

5	  title={Sim2Real-AD: A Modular Sim-to-Real Framework for Deploying VLM-Guided Reinforcement Learning in Real-World Autonomous Driving},

14	  abstract={Deploying reinforcement learning policies trained in simulation to real autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge, particularly for VLM-guided RL frameworks whose policies are typically learned with simulatornative observations and simulator-coupled action semantics that are unavailable on physical platforms. This paper
15	presents Sim2Real-AD, a modular framework for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer of CARLA-trained VLM-guided RL

22	@article{huang2026drivevlmrl,
23	  title={DriveVLM-RL: Neuroscience-Inspired Reinforcement Learning with Vision-Language Models for Safe and Deployable Autonomous Driving},
24	  author={Huang, Zilin and Sheng, Zihao and Wan, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and You, Junwei and Jiang, Sicong and Chen✉, Sikai},
25	  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.18315},
26	  year={2026},
27	  bibtex_show={true},
28	  abbr={arXiv},
29	  priority={0},
30	  google_scholar_id={Y0pCki6q_DkC},
31	  preview={drivevlmrl.webp},
32	  abstract={Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility.},
33	  annotation={* Equal Contribution<br>✉ Corresponding Author},
34	  selected={true}
35	}
36	
37	
38	@article{huang2025sky,
39	  title={Sky-Drive: A Distributed Multi-Agent Simulation Platform for Socially-Aware and Human-AI Collaborative Future Transportation},
40	  author={Huang*, Zilin and Sheng*, Zihao and Wan*, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and Luo, Yuhao and Wang, Boyue and Li, Pei and Chen, Yen-Jung and Chen, Jiancong and Long, Keke and Meng, Jiayi and Leng, Yue and Chen✉, Sikai},
41	  journal={Journal of Intelligent and Connected Vehicles},
42	  year={2025},
43	  bibtex_show={true},  
44	  abbr={JICV},
45	  priority={1},
46	  year={2025},
47	  abstract={Recent advances in autonomous system simulation platforms have significantly enhanced the safe and scalable testing of driving policies. However, existing simulators do not yet fully meet the needs of future transportation research, particularly in modeling socially-aware driving agents and enabling effective human-AI collaboration. This paper introduces Sky-Drive, a novel distributed multi-agent simulation platform that addresses these limitations through four key innovations: (a) a distributed architecture for synchronized simulation across multiple terminals; (b) a multi-modal human-in-the-loop framework integrating diverse sensors to collect rich behavioral data; (c) a human-AI collaboration mechanism supporting continuous and adaptive knowledge exchange; and (d) a digital twin (DT) framework for constructing high-fidelity virtual replicas of real-world transportation environments. Sky-Drive supports diverse applications such as autonomous vehicle (AV)-vulnerable road user (VRU) interaction modeling, human-in-the-loop training, socially-aware reinforcement learning, personalized driving policy, and customized scenario generation. Future extensions will incorporate foundation models for context-aware decision support and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for real-world validation. By bridging scenario generation, data collection, algorithm training, and hardware integration, Sky-Drive has the potential to become a foundational platform for the next generation of socially-aware and human-centered autonomous transportation researc},
48	  html={https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/Sky-Drive-website/},
49	  url={https://www.sciopen.com/article/10.26599/JICV.2026.9210070},
50	  google_scholar_id={9yKSN-GCB0IC},
51	  github_repo={BillWan-zzzyyy/Sky-Drive},
52	  video={https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/Sky-Drive-website/},
53	  preview={skydrive_new.webp},
54	  annotation={* Equal Contribution<br>✉ Corresponding Author},
55	  selected={true}
56	}

58	
59	@article{hechao2024tirewear,
60	  bibtex_show={true},  
61	  abbr={J. Automob. Eng.},
62	  priority={2},
63	  title={Study of the Tire Wear of Virtual Track Train},
64	  author={Zhou, Hechao and Wan, Zhengyang and Mei, Mingsu and Zhang✉, Jimin and Hecht, Markus},
65	  abstract={The Virtual Track Train (VTT) represents a novel urban transportation solution that combines elements of both road and rail vehicles. Currently, VTT has gained significant adoption in several cities in China including Yibin, Zhuzhou, Harbin, and Yancheng. Despite its growing usage, the unique structure and complex tire-ground interaction of VTT pose significant challenges in terms of tire wear, which has become a pressing issue. This study develops and validates a multi-body dynamics model of VTT along with a finite element model of its tire. The tire-ground contact behavior and boundary conditions are studied from various perspectives to calculate tire wear. The simulation results demonstrate that the internal pressure of the tires, the load of VTT, the pavement conditions, the inclination angle, and slippage of tires all have critical effects on VTT’s tire wear. These findings suggest that the operating conditions of VTT must be carefully managed to reduce tire wear and ensure safe operation. Furthermore, the research results of tire wear mechanism of VTT offers valuable insights for the optimization and maintenance of VTT.},
66	  journal={Proceedings of the Institution of Mechanical Engineers, Part D: Journal of Automobile Engineering},
67	  volume={239},
68	  issue={10-11},
69	  pages={4608--4621},

85	  bibtex_show={true},   
86	  abbr={CSCD},
87	  priority={2},
88	  title={Development of Driving Simulation Platform for Virtual Track Train},
89	  author={Wan, Zhengyang and Zhou, Hechao and Zhang✉, Jimin},
90	  abstract={The Virtual track train (VTT) has been put into commercial operation as a new type of urban rail transport in China in recent years. To further understand VTT’s operation performance, it is essential to develop a specific driving simulation platform to simulate VTT’s unique hybrid driving mode. The main contribution of this paper are as follows: First, a dynamics model of VTT was established using SIMAPCK. Then, the control module for simulating the hybrid driving mode of VTT was developed based on the model predictive control and Logitech driving simulation hardware. Furthermore, a driving scene visualization interface was established using Unreal Engine and Blender to simulate the real driving environment. These contents constitute the driving simulation platform for VTT. The simulation results show that the developed platform can deliver accurate and comprehensive operation performance of VTT under different driving modes, which has strong engineering significance.},

129	bing_site_verification: # out your bing-site-verification ID (Bing Webmaster)
130	
131	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# Blog
133	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	blog_name: al-folio # blog_name will be displayed in your blog page
136	blog_description: a simple whitespace theme for academics
137	permalink: /blog/:year/:title/
138	lsi: false # produce an index for related posts
139	
140	# Pagination
141	pagination:
142	  enabled: true
143	
144	related_blog_posts:
145	  enabled: true
146	  max_related: 10
147	
148	# Giscus comments (RECOMMENDED)
149	# Follow instructions on https://giscus.app/ to setup for your repo to fill out the information below.
150	giscus:
151	  repo: # <your-github-user-name>/<your-github-repo-name>
152	  repo_id: # leave empty or specify your repo_id (see https://giscus.app/)
153	  category: Comments # name of the category under which discussions will be created
154	  category_id: # leave empty or specify your category_id (see https://giscus.app/)
155	  mapping: title # identify discussions by post title
156	  strict: 1 # use strict identification mode
157	  reactions_enabled: 1 # enable (1) or disable (0) emoji reactions
158	  input_position: bottom # whether to display input form below (bottom) or above (top) the comments
159	  theme: preferred_color_scheme # name of the color scheme (preferred works well with al-folio light/dark mode)
160	  emit_metadata: 0
161	  lang: en
162	
163	# Disqus comments (DEPRECATED)
164	disqus_shortname: al-folio # put your disqus shortname
165	# https://help.disqus.com/en/articles/1717111-what-s-a-shortname
166	
167	# External sources.
168	# If you have blog posts published on medium.com or other external sources,
169	# you can display them in your blog by adding a link to the RSS feed.
170	#external_sources:
171	#  - name: medium.com
172	 #   rss_url: https://medium.com/@al-folio/feed
173	#  - name: Google Blog
174	  #  posts:
175	   #   - url: https://blog.google/technology/ai/google-gemini-update-flash-ai-assistant-io-2024/
176	    #    published_date: 2024-05-14
177	
178	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	# Newsletter
180	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	
182	newsletter:
183	  enabled: false
184	  endpoint: # your loops endpoint (e.g., https://app.loops.so/api/newsletter-form/YOUR-ENDPOINT)
185	  # https://loops.so/docs/forms/custom-form
186	
187	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
188	# Collections
189	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	
191	collections:
192	  news:
193	    defaults:
194	      layout: post
195	    output: true
196	    permalink: /:collection/:title/
197	  projects:
198	    output: true
199	    permalink: /:collection/:title/
200	
201	announcements:
202	  enabled: true
203	  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 news items
204	  limit: 7 # leave blank to include all the news in the `_news` folder
205	
206	latest_posts:
207	  enabled: true
208	  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 new posts items
209	  limit: 3 # leave blank to include all the blog posts
210	
211	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	# Jekyll settings
213	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	# Markdown and syntax highlight
216	markdown: kramdown
217	highlighter: rouge
218	kramdown:
219	  input: GFM
220	  syntax_highlighter_opts:
221	    css_class: "highlight"
222	    span:
223	      line_numbers: false
224	    block:
225	      line_numbers: false
226	      start_line: 1
227	
228	# Includes & excludes
229	include: ["_pages"]
230	exclude:
231	  - bin/
232	  - CONTRIBUTING.md
233	  - CUSTOMIZE.md
234	  - Dockerfile
235	  - docker-compose.yml
236	  - docker-compose-slim.yml
237	  - FAQ.md
238	  - Gemfile
239	  - Gemfile.lock
240	  - INSTALL.md
241	  - LICENSE
242	  - lighthouse_results/
243	  - package.json
244	  - package-lock.json
245	  - _pages/about_einstein.md
246	  - purgecss.config.js
247	  - README.md
248	  - readme_preview/
249	  - vendor
250	keep_files:
251	  - CNAME
252	  - .nojekyll
253	
254	# Plug-ins
255	plugins:
256	  - jekyll-archives
257	  - jekyll-email-protect
258	  - jekyll-feed
259	  - jekyll-get-json
260	  - jekyll-imagemagick
261	  - jekyll-jupyter-notebook
262	  - jekyll-link-attributes
263	  - jekyll-minifier
264	  - jekyll-paginate-v2
265	  - jekyll-regex-replace
266	  - jekyll/scholar
267	  - jekyll-sitemap
268	  - jekyll-tabs
269	  - jekyll-toc
270	  - jekyll-twitter-plugin
271	  - jemoji
272	
273	# Sitemap settings
274	defaults:
275	  - scope:
276	      path: "assets"
277	    values:
278	      sitemap: false
279	
280	sass:
281	  style: compressed
282	
283	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Jekyll Minifier
285	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	
287	jekyll-minifier:
288	  exclude: ["robots.txt", "assets/js/search/*.js", "assets/libs/**/*"]
289	  uglifier_args:
290	    harmony: true
291	
292	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
293	# Jekyll Archives
294	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
295	
296	jekyll-archives:
297	  enabled: [year, tags, categories] # enables year, tag and category archives (remove if you need to disable one of them).
298	  layouts:
299	    year: archive-year
300	    tag: archive-tag
301	    category: archive-category
302	  permalinks:
303	    year: "/blog/:year/"
304	    tag: "/blog/tag/:name/"
305	    category: "/blog/category/:name/"
306	
307	display_tags: ["formatting", "images", "links", "math", "code", "blockquotes"] # these tags will be displayed on the front page of your blog
308	display_categories: ["external-services"] # these categories will be displayed on the front page of your blog
309	
310	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	# Jekyll Scholar
312	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
313	
314	scholar:
315	  last_name: [Wan]
316	  first_name: [Zhengyang]
317	
318	  style: apa
319	  locale: en
320	
321	  source: /_bibliography/
322	  bibliography: papers.bib
323	  bibliography_template: bib
324	  # Note: if you have latex math in your bibtex, the latex filter
325	  # preprocessing may conflict with MathJAX if the latter is enabled.
326	  # See https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/357.
327	  bibtex_filters: [latex, smallcaps, superscript]
328	
329	  replace_strings: true
330	  join_strings: true
331	
332	  details_dir: bibliography
333	  details_link: Details
334	
335	  query: "@*"
336	  group_by: year
337	  group_order: descending
338	
339	# Display different badges withs stats for your publications
340	# Customize badge behavior in _layouts/bib.liquid
341	enable_publication_badges:
342	  altmetric: true # Altmetric badge (Customization options: https://badge-docs.altmetric.com/index.html)
343	  dimensions: true # Dimensions badge (Customization options: https://badge.dimensions.ai/)
344	  google_scholar: true # Google Scholar badge (https://scholar.google.com/intl/en/scholar/citations.html)
345	  github_stars: true # GitHub stars badge for papers with associated repos
346	  inspirehep: true # Inspire HEP badge (https://help.inspirehep.net/knowledge-base/citation-metrics/)
347	
348	# Filter out certain bibtex entry keywords used internally from the bib output
349	filtered_bibtex_keywords:
350	  [
351	    abbr,
352	    abstract,
353	    additional_info,
354	    altmetric,
355	    annotation,
356	    arxiv,
357	    award,
358	    award_name,
359	    bibtex_show,
360	    blog,
361	    code,
362	    github_repo,
363	    google_scholar_id,
364	    html,
365	    inspirehep_id,
366	    pdf,
367	    poster,
368	    preview,
369	    selected,
370	    slides,
371	    supp,
372	    video,
373	    website,
374	  ]
375	
376	# Maximum number of authors to be shown for each publication (more authors are visible on click)
377	max_author_limit: 3 # leave blank to always show all authors
378	more_authors_animation_delay: 10 # more authors are revealed on click using animation; smaller delay means faster animation
379	
380	# Enables publication thumbnails. If disabled, none of the publications will display thumbnails, even if specified in the bib entry.
381	enable_publication_thumbnails: true
382	
383	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	# Jekyll Link Attributes
385	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	
387	# These are the defaults
388	external_links:
389	  enabled: true
390	  rel: external nofollow noopener
391	  target: _blank
392	  exclude:
393	
394	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
395	# Responsive WebP Images
396	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
397	
398	# MAKE SURE imagemagick is installed and on your PATH before enabling imagemagick. In a terminal, run:
399	#   convert -version
400	imagemagick:
401	  enabled: true # enables responsive images for your site (recommended, see https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/537)
402	  widths:
403	    - 480
404	    - 800
405	    - 1400
406	  input_directories:
407	    - assets/img/
408	  input_formats:
409	    - ".jpg"
410	    - ".jpeg"
411	    - ".png"
412	    - ".tiff"
413	    - ".gif"
414	  output_formats:
415	    webp: "-quality 85"
416	
417	# Lazy loading images
418	#   If you enable lazy loading, all images will add the loading="lazy" attribute.
419	#   This will make your site load faster, but it may not be supported in all browsers.
420	#   You can also set loading="" to other values for specific images to override the default behavior.
421	#   Options: "auto", "eager", "lazy"
422	#   See https://web.dev/browser-level-image-lazy-loading/ for more information.
423	lazy_loading_images: true # enables lazy loading of images (recommended)
424	
425	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
426	# Optional Features
427	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
428	
429	enable_google_analytics: false # enables google analytics
430	enable_cronitor_analytics: false # enables cronitor RUM analytics
431	enable_pirsch_analytics: false # enables Pirsch analytics (https://pirsch.io/)
432	enable_openpanel_analytics: false # enables Openpanel analytics (https://openpanel.dev/)
433	enable_google_verification: false # enables google site verification
434	enable_bing_verification: false # enables bing site verification
435	enable_masonry: true # enables automatic project cards arrangement
436	enable_math: true # enables math typesetting (uses MathJax)
437	enable_tooltips: false # enables automatic tooltip links generated for each section titles on pages and posts
438	enable_darkmode: true # enables switching between light/dark modes
439	enable_navbar_social: false # enables displaying social links in the navbar on the about page
440	enable_project_categories: true # enables categorization of projects into multiple categories
441	enable_medium_zoom: true # enables image zoom feature (as on medium.com)
442	enable_progressbar: true # enables a horizontal progress bar linked to the vertical scroll position
443	enable_video_embedding: false # enables video embedding for bibtex entries. If false, the button opens the video link in a new window.
444

## Font System Analysis for al-folio Jekyll Theme

Based on my exploration, here's the complete font system configuration:

### 1. **Current Fonts Being Used**

All fonts are defined in `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_sass/_base.scss`:

- **Body text** (line 8): `'Crimson Pro', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif`
- **Headings** (h1-h6) (line 12): `'Inter', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
- **Code/monospace** (line 16): `'JetBrains Mono', SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace`

### 2. **Google Fonts Configuration**

The fonts are loaded via Google Fonts API. The URL is configured in `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_config.yml` at **line 494**:

```
fonts: "https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Crimson+Pro:400,500,600,700|Inter:400,500,600,700|JetBrains+Mono:400,500|Material+Icons&display=swap"
```

This is referenced in the template file `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_includes/head.liquid` at **line 30**, where it loads:

```liquid
<link defer rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="{{ site.third_party_libraries.google_fonts.url.fonts }}">
```

### 3. **Key Files and Line Numbers**

| File | Line | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `_sass/_base.scss` | 8 | Body font-family: Crimson Pro |
| `_sass/_base.scss` | 12 | Heading font-family: Inter |
| `_sass/_base.scss` | 16 | Code/monospace font-family: JetBrains Mono |
| `_sass/_base.scss` | 238 | Profile .more-info section uses monospace |
| `_config.yml` | 493-494 | Google Fonts URL configuration (third_party_libraries.google_fonts) |
| `_includes/head.liquid` | 30 | Font stylesheet link loader |

### 4. **Font Weights Available**

From the Google Fonts URL, the current setup loads:
- **Crimson Pro**: 400, 500, 600, 700
- **Inter**: 400, 500, 600, 700
- **JetBrains Mono**: 400, 500
- **Material Icons**: icons

### 5. **Variable/Configuration Files**

- `_sass/_variables.scss`: Stores colors and theme variables (lines 1-59), but **no font variables are centralized here**
- `_config.yml`: All font configuration is under `third_party_libraries.google_fonts` (lines 492-494)

### Summary

The font system is **straightforward**:
1. Google Fonts URL is configured in `_config.yml`
2. Fonts are loaded in `_includes/head.liquid`
3. Font-family declarations are hardcoded in `_sass/_base.scss`

To change to Claude.ai fonts, you would need to:
1. Update the Google Fonts URL in `_config.yml` (line 494)
2. Update the three font-family declarations in `_sass/_base.scss` (lines 8, 12, and 16)

6		    keywords.each do |keyword|

## Summary: Author Name Rendering in Jekyll al-folio Bibliography

Based on my thorough exploration of your Jekyll academic website, here's how author names flow from the .bib file to rendered HTML:

### 1. **Bibliography Source File**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_bibliography/papers.bib`

Author names are stored in standard BibTeX format:
- Lines 6, 24, 40, 64, 89: Author names appear in the `author={}` field
- Format examples:
  - `author={Huang, Zilin and Wan, Zhengyang and Sheng, Zihao and ...}`
  - `author={Wan, Zhengyang and Zhou, Hechao and Zhang✉, Jimin}`
- Metadata annotations use symbols: `*` for equal contribution, `✉` for corresponding author

### 2. **Jekyll Scholar Configuration**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_config.yml` (lines 314-337)

The scholar configuration identifies **your name** for self-highlighting:
```yaml
scholar:
  last_name: [Wan]
  first_name: [Zhengyang]
  
  style: apa
  locale: en
  source: /_bibliography/
  bibliography: papers.bib
  bibliography_template: bib
```

Additional settings affecting author display:
- Line 377: `max_author_limit: 3` — Limits visible authors before "more authors" link
- Line 378: `more_authors_animation_delay: 10` — Animation speed for author list reveal

### 3. **Core Author Rendering Logic**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 54-148)

The rendering flow:

**Lines 63-70:** Self-identification logic
```liquid
{% assign author_is_self = false %}
{%- assign author_last_name = author.last | regex_replace: '[*∗†‡§¶‖&^]', '' -%}
{% if site.scholar.last_name contains author_last_name %}
  {% if site.scholar.first_name contains author.first %}
    {% assign author_is_self = true %}
  {% endif %}
{% endif %}
```
- Strips symbols from author last names for comparison
- Checks if author matches `site.scholar.last_name` (Wan) AND `site.scholar.first_name` (Zhengyang)

**Lines 71-80:** Coauthor profile URL lookup
```liquid
{% assign coauthor_url = null %}
{%- assign clean_last_name = author_last_name | downcase | remove_accents -%}
{% if site.data.coauthors[clean_last_name] %}
  {%- for coauthor in site.data.coauthors[clean_last_name] -%}
    {% if coauthor.firstname contains author.first %}
      {%- assign coauthor_url = coauthor.url -%}
    {% endif %}
  {% endfor %}
{% endif %}
```
- Looks up author profiles in `_data/coauthors.yml`

**Lines 86-101:** Conditional rendering
```liquid
{%- if author_is_self -%}
  <em>{{- author.first }} {{ author_last_html -}}</em>
{%- else -%}
  {%- if coauthor_url -%}
    <a href="{{ coauthor_url }}">{{- author.first }} {{ author_last_html -}}</a>
  {%- else -%}
    {{- author.first }} {{ author_last_html -}}
  {% endif %}
{%- endif -%}
```
- **Self (Zhengyang Wan):** Rendered in `<em>` tags (italics)
- **Known coauthors:** Rendered as `<a>` links to their profiles
- **Unknown authors:** Plain text

**Lines 103-137:** "More authors" collapsible list for when `max_author_limit` is exceeded

### 4. **Coauthor Data File**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_data/coauthors.yml`

Currently only contains example historical figures (Einstein, Bach, Planck, etc.). Your coauthors are NOT yet configured here, so they render as plain text. To add clickable links for coauthors, entries would look like:

```yaml
"wan":
  - firstname: ["Zhengyang", "Z."]
    url: https://billwan-zzzyyy.github.io/

"huang":
  - firstname: ["Zilin", "Z."]
    url: https://example.com/zilin-huang
```

### 5. **Symbol Handling**
**Paths:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_plugins/hide-custom-bibtex.rb` (lines 10-11)

Superscript symbols (`*†‡§¶‖&^`) are:
- Stripped during self-name comparison (line 64 of bib.liquid)
- Converted to `<sup>` HTML tags for display (line 65 of bib.liquid)
- Removed from BibTeX output blocks (hide-custom-bibtex.rb)

### 6. **CSS Styling**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_sass/_base.scss`

```scss
.author {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  
  a {
    border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-theme-color);
    &:hover { border-bottom-style: solid; }

- Self author (`<em>`): italicized with solid underline

### 7. **Publications Page Entry Point**
**Path:** `/d/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_pages/publications.md` (line 41)

```liquid
{% bibliography %}
```
Uses jekyll-scholar gem to render entries via `bib.liquid` template

### Key Flow Summary:
1. **BibTeX parsing:** jekyll-scholar parses `papers.bib` 
2. **Self-detection:** bib.liquid compares each author against `_config.yml` scholar settings
3. **Profile lookup:** Attempts to find author URLs in `_data/coauthors.yml`
4. **Rendering:** Outputs `<em>` (self), `<a>` (linked coauthors), or plain text
5. **Styling:** CSS applies different borders/underlines based on element type
6. **Symbol handling:** Superscripts for contributions/affiliation are preserved visually

Your current setup automatically italicizes and underlines **Zhengyang Wan** on all publications. To bold your name instead, you would modify the `<em>` rendering in bib.liquid (line 87-90) to use `<strong>` tags or add a custom CSS class.

**Claude.ai 使用的正文字体是商业字体 "Copernicus"（也叫 Anthropic Serif），无法免费获取。你希望用哪种方式来接近 Claude 的字体风格？**

Options:
- **系统衬线字体栈 (推荐)** - 使用与 Claude.ai 相同的 fallback 字体栈：ui-serif, Georgia, Cambria, 'Times New Roman', serif。无需加载额外字体，加载最快，效果最接近 Claude。
- **Source Serif 4 (Google Fonts)** - Adobe 开源衬线字体，风格与 Copernicus 最接近，优雅的过渡衬线体。需要从 Google Fonts 加载。
- **保留当前 Crimson Pro** - 不更改字体，只执行作者名加粗的修改。


**Answer:** Source Serif 4 (Google Fonts)" selected preview:
body {
  font-family: 'Source Serif 4', ui-serif,
    Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;
}

# Plan: Bold Author Name + Change Font to Source Serif 4

## Context
User wants two changes to their al-folio academic website:
1. Bold their name (Zhengyang Wan) in the publications list for better visibility
2. Change the body font from Crimson Pro to Source Serif 4 (closest free alternative to Claude.ai's Copernicus font)

## Changes

### 1. Bold author name in publications
**File:** `_sass/_base.scss` (line 783)

Change `.author > em` style from `font-weight: 400` to `font-weight: bold`.

Currently the self-author name is rendered in `<em>` tags (via `_layouts/bib.liquid:86-90`) with CSS that removes italic and adds an underline. Adding bold will make it stand out more while keeping the underline.

\```diff
 > em {
-  font-weight: 400;
+  font-weight: bold;
   border-bottom: 1px solid;
   font-style: normal;
 }
\```

### 2. Change body font to Source Serif 4
**File 1:** `_config.yml` (line 494)

Replace `Crimson+Pro:400,500,600,700` with `Source+Serif+4:400,500,600,700` in the Google Fonts URL.

**File 2:** `_sass/_base.scss` (line 8)

Change body font-family:
\```diff
-font-family: 'Crimson Pro', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;
+font-family: 'Source Serif 4', ui-serif, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;
\```

## Verification
1. Run `bundle exec jekyll serve` locally and check:
   - Publications page: author name "Zhengyang Wan" should appear **bold** with underline
   - All pages: body text should render in Source Serif 4 font
   - Headings should remain in Inter (unchanged)
   - Code blocks should remain in JetBrains Mono (unchanged)

## Approved Plan:
# Plan: Bold Author Name + Change Font to Source Serif 4

## Context
User wants two changes to their al-folio academic website:
1. Bold their name (Zhengyang Wan) in the publications list for better visibility
2. Change the body font from Crimson Pro to Source Serif 4 (closest free alternative to Claude.ai's Copernicus font)

## Changes

### 1. Bold author name in publications
**File:** `_sass/_base.scss` (line 783)

Change `.author > em` style from `font-weight: 400` to `font-weight: bold`.

Currently the self-author name is rendered in `<em>` tags (via `_layouts/bib.liquid:86-90`) with CSS that removes italic and adds an underline. Adding bold will make it stand out more while keeping the underline.

```diff
 > em {
-  font-weight: 400;
+  font-weight: bold;
   border-bottom: 1px solid;
   font-style: normal;
 }
```

### 2. Change body font to Source Serif 4
**File 1:** `_config.yml` (line 494)

Replace `Crimson+Pro:400,500,600,700` with `Source+Serif+4:400,500,600,700` in the Google Fonts URL.

**File 2:** `_sass/_base.scss` (line 8)

Change body font-family:
```diff
-font-family: 'Crimson Pro', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;
+font-family: 'Source Serif 4', ui-serif, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;
```

## Verification
1. Run `bundle exec jekyll serve` locally and check:
   - Publications page: author name "Zhengyang Wan" should appear **bold** with underline
   - All pages: body text should render in Source Serif 4 font
   - Headings should remain in Inter (unchanged)
   - Code blocks should remain in JetBrains Mono (unchanged)
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Convert animated GIF to animated WebP

```bash
cd "D:/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io" && python -c "
from PIL import Image
import os

gif = Image.open('assets/img/publication_preview/skydrive_new.gif')
frames = []
durations = []
for i in range(gif.n_frames):
    gif.seek(i)
    frame = gif.convert('RGBA')
    frames.append(frame)
    durations.append(gif.info.get('duration', 30))

frames[0].save(
    'assets/img/publication_preview/skydrive_new.webp',
    save_all=True,
    append_images=frames[1:],
    duration=durations,
    loop=0,
    quality=80,
    method=4
)

size_gif = os.path.getsize('assets/img/publication_preview/skydrive_new.gif') / 1024
size_webp = os.path.getsize('assets/img/publication_preview/skydrive_new.webp') / 1024
print(f'GIF:  {size_gif:.0f} KB')
print(f'WebP: {size_webp:.0f} KB')
print(f'Compression: {size_webp/size_gif*100:.1f}%')
" 2>&1
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 03:53:09Z)**_

3.2 MB 的确偏大。预览图显示宽度只有 280px，但原图是 600px 宽，而且有 300 帧。让我缩小分辨率 + 降低帧数来压缩。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 03:53:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Resize and reduce frames of GIF

```bash
cd "D:/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io" && python -c "
from PIL import Image
import os, tempfile

gif = Image.open('assets/img/publication_preview/skydrive_new.gif')
print(f'Original: {gif.size}, {gif.n_frames} frames, duration={gif.info.get(\"duration\")}ms')

# Resize to 400px wide (enough for 280px display + retina)
new_w = 400
ratio = new_w / gif.size[0]
new_h = int(gif.size[1] * ratio)

# Extract every 2nd frame (150 frames instead of 300, double duration)
frames = []
durations = []
for i in range(0, gif.n_frames, 2):
    gif.seek(i)
    frame = gif.convert('RGBA').resize((new_w, new_h), Image.LANCZOS)
    frames.append(frame)
    durations.append(gif.info.get('duration', 30) * 2)

# Save as temp GIF first, then convert with gif2webp
temp_gif = 'assets/img/publication_preview/skydrive_temp.gif'
frames[0].save(
    temp_gif,
    save_all=True,
    append_images=frames[1:],
    duration=durations,
    loop=0,
    optimize=True
)
print(f'Resized GIF: {new_w}x{new_h}, {len(frames)} frames, {os.path.getsize(temp_gif)/1024:.0f} KB')
" 2>&1
```

```text
Original: (600, 364), 300 frames, duration=30ms
Resized GIF: 400x242, 150 frames, 7554 KB
[rerun: b18]
```
</details></tool-use>
